Pressure Washing in Kansas City

Professional pressure washing and soft washing for siding, driveways, decks, and patios — restoring your home's curb appeal in a single visit.

Get a Free Quote (816) 539-5496

Years of dirt, algae, pollen, and grime build up on every exterior surface of your home, and standard rinsing won't touch it. Our pressure washing crews use commercial-grade equipment and the right technique for each surface — high-pressure water for concrete and hard surfaces, and gentle soft washing with cleaning solution for siding, roofs, and anything that could be damaged by too much force. The result is a home that looks freshly built, without the risk of stripped paint, etched concrete, or damaged siding that comes with a rented pressure washer and a weekend DIY attempt.

What's included

  • Free on-site estimate and surface assessment
  • Driveway, sidewalk, and patio concrete cleaning
  • House siding wash (vinyl, brick, stucco, or fiber cement)
  • Deck and fence cleaning and brightening
  • Soft washing for roofs and delicate surfaces
  • Gutter exterior and downspout rinse
  • Pre- and post-job photos so you can see the difference

Why homeowners choose us

  • Licensed, insured, and background-checked technicians
  • Commercial-grade equipment gets the job done faster and safer than rental units
  • Soft washing option protects paint, caulking, and landscaping
  • Eco-conscious cleaning solutions that are safe for plants and pets once dry
  • Flat, upfront pricing with no surprise add-ons
  • Satisfaction guarantee on every job

Our process

  1. Free estimate — We assess your surfaces and walk you through what pressure washing vs. soft washing will accomplish.
  2. Pre-treatment — Heavily soiled areas get a pre-treatment application to break down algae, mildew, and grime before rinsing.
  3. Wash — We clean each surface at the correct pressure and technique — high pressure for concrete, low pressure for siding and roofing.
  4. Rinse & inspect — A final rinse removes all residue, and we walk the property with you to confirm everything meets your expectations.

Frequently asked questions

What's the difference between pressure washing and soft washing?

Pressure washing uses high-pressure water alone to blast away dirt and is best for tough, durable surfaces like concrete and brick. Soft washing uses a lower-pressure spray combined with a specialized cleaning solution to safely lift algae, mold, and grime from more delicate surfaces like siding and roofing without causing damage.

How often should I get my house pressure washed?

Most Kansas City homes benefit from a professional wash once a year, though homes shaded by trees or near high humidity may need it every 6 to 9 months to stay ahead of algae and mildew growth.

Is pressure washing safe for my landscaping?

Yes. We take care to protect nearby plants, cover sensitive vegetation when needed, and use cleaning solutions that are safe for your yard once they've been rinsed and dried.

How much does pressure washing cost in Kansas City?

Pricing depends on square footage, surface type, and how much buildup is present. We provide a free, no-obligation quote after a quick on-site or photo-based assessment.

Can pressure washing damage my siding or driveway?

Improper pressure washing can, which is why we match the pressure and technique to each surface. Soft washing is used on siding and roofing specifically to avoid the damage a too-aggressive spray can cause.

Handyman Services in Kansas City

Skilled handymen for repairs, installations, and multi-item punch lists across the Kansas City metro — done right the first time.

Get a Free Quote (816) 539-5496

From a single leaky faucet to a full weekend punch list, our handyman team handles the jobs that pile up on your to-do list. We send experienced, background-checked pros who show up on time, bring the right tools, and treat your home with respect. Whether it's drywall repair, fixture installation, carpentry, or a list of ten small fixes you've been putting off, we bundle the work into one visit so you're not scheduling five different contractors.

What's included

  • Drywall repair and patching
  • Interior and exterior painting touch-ups
  • Fixture, faucet, and hardware installation
  • Door and window repair or adjustment
  • Carpentry and trim work
  • Furniture assembly and mounting (TVs, shelves, mirrors)
  • Multi-item punch lists bundled into a single visit

Why homeowners choose us

  • One trusted pro for your whole list instead of five different contractors
  • Transparent, upfront pricing before work begins
  • Background-checked, experienced technicians
  • Fully insured for your peace of mind
  • Clean, respectful work in your home — we clean up after ourselves
  • Satisfaction guarantee on every job

Our process

  1. Tell us your list — Share the repairs or projects you need done — big or small — and we'll give you a free estimate.
  2. Schedule your visit — We book a time that works for you and send a technician with the right tools and materials.
  3. Work gets done — Your handyman completes the job list efficiently, checking in with you along the way on anything unexpected.
  4. Final walkthrough — We review the completed work together before we consider the job done.

Frequently asked questions

What kind of jobs can your handymen handle?

Most interior and exterior home repairs and installations that don't require a specialty licensed trade — drywall, painting, carpentry, fixture installs, minor plumbing and electrical fixture swaps, and general punch-list items.

Do I need to have a big list of repairs to book a handyman?

Not at all. We're happy to help with a single repair, though bundling several small jobs into one visit is often the most efficient and cost-effective way to use your appointment.

How is handyman pricing determined?

We provide a free estimate based on the scope of work, whether that's an hourly rate for a mixed punch list or a flat rate for a defined project.

Are your handymen insured?

Yes, all of our technicians are background-checked and our work is fully insured.

Mobile Car Repair in Kansas City

On-site auto repair and maintenance that comes to your driveway — no towing, no waiting rooms, no wasted afternoons.

Get a Free Quote (816) 539-5496

Car trouble doesn't have to mean a half-day sitting in a waiting room. Our mobile mechanics come to your home or office throughout the Kansas City metro and handle common repairs and maintenance right in your driveway. You get the same quality work as a traditional shop, without the tow, the drop-off, or the schedule disruption.

What's included

  • Battery testing and replacement
  • Brake pad and rotor service
  • Oil changes and fluid top-offs
  • Starter and alternator diagnostics and replacement
  • Pre-purchase and general vehicle inspections
  • Check engine light diagnostics

Why homeowners choose us

  • No towing or drop-off required — we come to you
  • Transparent pricing quoted before any work begins
  • ASE-familiar technicians using quality parts
  • Same-week scheduling for most repairs
  • Save a trip: work from home or run errands while we work
  • Warranty on parts and labor

Our process

  1. Describe the issue — Tell us your symptoms, vehicle year/make/model, or the maintenance you need.
  2. Get a quote — We provide an estimate and schedule a visit at your home or office.
  3. On-site repair — Our mobile mechanic arrives with the parts and tools to complete the job in your driveway.
  4. Drive away confident — We test the repair, walk you through what was done, and back the work with a warranty.

Frequently asked questions

What repairs can a mobile mechanic actually do in my driveway?

Most maintenance and common repairs — brakes, batteries, starters, alternators, fluid services, and diagnostics — can be completed on-site. More extensive engine or transmission work may still require a shop, and we'll let you know upfront if that's the case.

Is mobile car repair more expensive than a shop?

Pricing is generally comparable to a traditional shop once you factor in the time and cost of towing or waiting on-site. We provide a clear quote before any work begins so there are no surprises.

What areas do you service?

We serve the Kansas City metro area, including Overland Park, Lee's Summit, North Kansas City, Shawnee, and Blue Springs.

Do you offer a warranty on mobile repairs?

Yes, parts and labor are backed by a warranty just as they would be at a traditional repair shop.

Gutter Cleaning in Kansas City

Thorough gutter and downspout cleaning that prevents water damage, foundation issues, and pest problems.

Clogged gutters send water pouring down your siding and pooling at your foundation instead of safely away from your home. Our upcoming gutter cleaning service will clear debris from gutters and downspouts, check for proper flow, and flag any repairs your gutter system may need.

Why it matters

  • Prevents water damage to siding, fascia, and foundation
  • Reduces standing water that attracts mosquitoes and pests
  • Extends the life of your gutter system
  • Pairs well with an annual pressure washing visit

Home Exterior Care · 9 min read

Pressure Washing vs. Soft Washing: Your Top Questions, Answered

Kansas City homeowners ask us some version of these questions every week. Here's the straight answer to each one, from what actually separates pressure washing from soft washing to how much it costs and how often you really need it.

In this guide

  1. Pressure washing vs. soft washing: what's the real difference?
  2. Which method does my home actually need?
  3. How often should I clean my home's exterior?
  4. Is this safe for my plants, pets, and kids?
  5. What does it cost, and is DIY worth it?
  6. Frequently asked questions

Pressure washing vs. soft washing: what's the real difference?

The two terms get used interchangeably, but they're different tools for different jobs. Pressure washing uses high-pressure water on its own — often 3,000 PSI or more — to physically blast dirt, algae, and stains off tough, durable surfaces. Soft washing uses much lower pressure, closer to a garden hose, paired with a specialized cleaning solution that breaks down organic growth like algae, mold, and mildew at the surface level.

In short: pressure washing scrubs with force, and soft washing cleans with chemistry. Neither one is universally "better" — the right choice depends entirely on the surface.

Which method does my home actually need?

As a rule of thumb, hard and durable surfaces do well with pressure washing, while softer or more delicate surfaces call for soft washing:

  • Pressure washing: concrete driveways and sidewalks, brick patios, retaining walls, most wood and composite decking.
  • Soft washing: vinyl and fiber-cement siding, stucco, painted wood trim, asphalt shingle roofs, window screens.

Many homes need both in the same visit — a soft wash on the siding and roofline, and a pressure wash on the driveway and patio. A professional assessment (free with our quote) is the fastest way to know exactly what your home needs.

How often should I clean my home's exterior?

Most Kansas City homes hold up well with one professional wash per year. If your home sits in heavy shade, near sprinklers, or in a naturally damp spot, algae and mildew can creep back faster, and a six-to-nine-month schedule may make more sense. Driveways and patios that see heavy vehicle traffic or grilling activity may also benefit from more frequent attention.

Is this safe for my plants, pets, and kids?

Done correctly, yes. Reputable soft washing uses a diluted cleaning solution, protects or pre-rinses nearby plants, and finishes with a thorough rinse so nothing harmful is left behind once everything dries. The main thing to watch for during the job itself is simply keeping people and pets clear of the active work area, the same way you would for any exterior maintenance.

What does it cost, and is DIY worth it?

Cost depends on square footage, the mix of surfaces, and how much buildup has accumulated over time. There's no single flat rate that applies to every home, which is why we quote each job individually after a quick look at the property. A rented pressure washer can seem like the cheaper route, but it's also the most common way homeowners end up etching concrete, stripping paint, or pushing water behind siding — damage that costs far more to fix than the wash itself would have. If you do go the DIY route, start with the lowest effective pressure and a wide fan tip, and reserve anything stronger for concrete only.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between pressure washing and soft washing?

Pressure washing relies on high-pressure water alone to blast dirt, algae, and grime off durable surfaces like concrete, brick, and stone. Soft washing uses a much lower water pressure combined with a specialized cleaning solution — usually a mix of water, a mild bleach-based algaecide, and surfactant — to break down and rinse away organic growth on delicate surfaces like vinyl siding, stucco, and roof shingles. The chemical does the cleaning in soft washing; the water pressure does the cleaning in pressure washing.

Which surfaces need soft washing instead of pressure washing?

Roofs, vinyl and fiber-cement siding, painted wood, stucco, and window screens are all better candidates for soft washing. High pressure aimed at asphalt shingles can lift or damage them, and it can drive water behind siding panels. Soft washing cleans these surfaces gently while still killing the algae and mildew at the root, so it grows back slower than after a straight rinse.

Which surfaces should be pressure washed instead?

Concrete driveways, sidewalks, brick patios, retaining walls, and most decking can handle and benefit from higher-pressure cleaning, since it's effective at lifting ground-in dirt, oil stains, and moss from hard, durable surfaces.

How often should I have my house washed?

Most Kansas City homes do well with a professional wash once a year. Homes shaded by trees, close to sprinklers, or in shadier, more humid spots may need attention every six to nine months to stay ahead of algae and mildew before it becomes visible.

Is soft washing safe for my landscaping, pets, and kids?

When applied correctly by a professional, yes. The cleaning solution is diluted to a safe concentration, plants are pre-rinsed or covered as needed, and the area is thoroughly rinsed afterward. Once the surfaces dry, it's safe for normal activity. It's still smart to keep pets and kids away from the work area while the crew is actively cleaning.

Will pressure washing damage my driveway or siding?

It can, if the pressure and nozzle aren't matched to the surface. Too much pressure aimed at aging mortar joints, painted wood, or siding seams can strip paint, etch concrete, or force water where it shouldn't go. This is exactly why professionals vary pressure and technique by surface instead of using one setting for the whole house.

How much does pressure washing or soft washing cost?

Cost depends on square footage, the surfaces involved, and how much buildup has accumulated. A small concrete patio costs far less than a full two-story soft wash. The most reliable way to get an accurate number is a free on-site or photo-based quote rather than a generic per-square-foot estimate.

Can I pressure wash my house myself with a rented machine?

You can, but it's the most common way homeowners accidentally damage siding, strip paint, or etch concrete, since consumer and rental units don't always give you fine control over pressure and pattern. If you do it yourself, start on a low-pressure, wide-fan setting and test an inconspicuous area first — or have a professional assess which surfaces genuinely need soft washing instead.

Does soft washing kill algae and mold, or just remove the visible growth?

A proper soft wash kills the organic growth at the surface, not just rinses off what's visible, which is why the results last significantly longer than a plain water rinse. That said, no exterior cleaning is permanent — shaded, damp areas will regrow algae faster than sun-exposed ones, which is part of why annual maintenance visits matter.

What's the best time of year to pressure wash or soft wash in Kansas City?

Spring and fall tend to be ideal — temperatures are mild enough for cleaning solutions to work effectively and for surfaces to dry without flash-freezing. That said, washing can be done most of the year as long as temperatures stay safely above freezing.

Ready for a spotless exterior?

Get a free, no-obligation quote for pressure washing or soft washing anywhere in the Kansas City metro.
